questions.question:
A StatefulSet named `echo-api` and Services `echo-api` and `echo-api-headless` in namespace `connectivity-lab` are not providing the intended in-cluster connectivity.

Fix the workload so that:
- Service `echo-api` stays `ClusterIP`
- Service `echo-api` selects pods with label `app=echo-api`
- Service `echo-api` exposes port `8080` and targets port `8080`
- Service `echo-api-headless` stays headless (`clusterIP: None`)
- Service `echo-api-headless` selects pods with label `app=echo-api`
- StatefulSet `echo-api` keeps `serviceName: echo-api-headless`
- StatefulSet `echo-api` keeps `replicas: 1`
- the StatefulSet becomes Ready
- `net-debug` can fetch `ok` from `http://echo-api:8080/healthz`
- `net-debug` can fetch `ok` from `http://echo-api-0.echo-api-headless.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local:8080/healthz`
- `net-debug` can resolve `echo-api.connectivity-lab.svc.cluster.local`

Do not delete the StatefulSet, do not create new Pods, and do not convert either Service into a NodePort or LoadBalancer.

verification.description: Services echo-api and echo-api-headless keep the intended selectors and ports while StatefulSet echo-api keeps the expected serviceName and replica count
StatefulSet echo-api becomes Ready and net-debug can resolve the Service name and fetch ok through Service echo-api
net-debug can fetch ok through the headless ordinal DNS name after the connectivity repair
